preamble: The shift from the palatal nasal [ɲ] to the alveolar nasal [n] is known as depalatalization. This process often occurs in word-final positions or as part of a general simplification of the consonant system, moving from a more marked palatal sound to a more common alveolar one.

examples.note: In many dialects, Spanish word-final [ɲ] (though rare and often appearing in loans or as a result of specific processes) is realized as or reduced to the alveolar [n].
Historical developments in Romanian saw several palatalized nasals depalatalizing to alveolar nasals.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
